key_insights.insight: Discipline is about prioritizing the needs of your future self over your present self.
key_insights.quote: Discipline as your ability to prioritize the needs of your future self ahead of your own present self. And that's it. That's all all discipline is.

key_insights.insight: Making decisions that prioritize your future self generates dopamine, which makes discipline easier.
key_insights.quote: If I can start looking backwards with gratitude, that's the fastest way to make discipline dopamine generating.

key_insights.insight: Focus on building habits that make your goals a byproduct.
key_insights.quote: All of our lives are about habits, not goals, but what are the habits that make my goal a byproduct?

key_insights.insight: The discipline needed to start a new habit is often small.
key_insights.quote: The discipline only is necessary. You only need like a teaspoon of it at the very beginning to get this habit started.

key_insights.insight: Vision boards help show the mammalian brain imagery that it understands, reinforcing goals.
key_insights.quote: But we're definitely showing something that a dog can understand. It's imagery. And dog can understand images.

key_insights.insight: Brainwashing yourself to form new habits involves focus, emotion, agitation (disrupting your environment), and repetition.
key_insights.quote: If you follow the brainwashing formula, which is focus, emotion, agitation, and repetition. It spells fear. And that is the best way to brainwash yourself to form these new habits and goals.

key_insights.insight: Disrupting your environment can prevent your brain from defaulting to old, unwanted scripts.
key_insights.quote: Agitation means I'm going to disrupt my environment so much and so often that my brain has no chance to default to an older script.

actionable_advice.advice: Prepare things the night before to lower the barrier to entry for morning routines.
actionable_advice.how_to: Lay out clothes, prepare the coffee maker, and gather everything needed for the day.

actionable_advice.advice: Create surprises for your future self to foster gratitude from your present self.
actionable_advice.how_to: Hide money in a jacket pocket you won't wear for a season or leave a positive Post-it note in an unexpected place.

actionable_advice.advice: Radically alter your environment to disrupt old habit patterns.
actionable_advice.how_to: Repaint your house, rearrange furniture, change your wardrobe, or get a new haircut.
Use a TV with a tablet duct-taped to the back running a slideshow of images representing your goals 24/7.

memorable_stories: The lady who tried to lose weight on YouTube but gained more instead because her actions contradicted her desire for change.
Simon Sinek's example of taking out the trash and applying the 'Why + Reinforcement - Cost' formula.
The client who was advised to get a 70-inch TV and a tablet to create a continuous vision board.
contrarian_claims: The best way to brainwash yourself into forming new habits is to use a formula of focus, emotion, agitation, and repetition.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Moment 203: How To Actually Become Disciplined WITHOUT Willpower…  The Leading Behaviour Expert
